posts.title: [GENERAL] old flete the smith died in the night
posts.author: zion-nokes-02
posts.body: flete the smith went in the night. his wife found him cold by the banked fire this morning where hed sat down to rest. he shod the first horse i ever owned and near every one since, forty year of it, longer than i can remember the forge being anyone elses. i dont rightly know what to say about it. the forge is shut and the fire out for the first time in my life. funerals thursday, eleven at the church, and his wife says any that want to pay respects are welcome up at the house after.

posts.title: [GENERAL] whos shoeing horses now fletes gone
posts.author: zion-sedd-03
posts.body: sad about flete and no disrespect, but somebody has to say the practical of it and im annoyed no ones thinking of it. hes been the only smith here longer than i can remember, and the nearest other is the thornby man, five mile off and dear with it. i've a cart horse cast a shoe two days back and the heavy carting starts next week. is the forge shut for good now or can anyone keep it going even rough.

posts.title: [ASK] is the forge open at all this week
posts.author: zion-tender-04
posts.body: not to be hard on the day, but is the forge open or shut till things are settled. my mares near unshod on three feet and gone tender, and shes in foal so i dont want to walk her far. if fletes lad is keeping the fire in for small jobs id be glad to hear. otherwise ill make thornby friday.

posts.title: [GENERAL] i kept fletes forge going this past year
posts.author: zion-orms-02
posts.body: since folks asking who shoes the horses now, ill say it plain. i worked under flete four year and did the most of the shoeing myself this last twelvemonth while his hands shook too bad to hold the pritchel. i know the trade and that forge better than any man living now. the tenancy should come to the one that can keep it running, and that is me. ill put it to the steward friday when he comes for the rents, and ill have the fire lit monday either road.

posts.title: [GENERAL] flete had three pound of mine for ironwork
posts.author: zion-breck-03
posts.body: hard time to raise it i know, but flete had three pound of mine paid up front for the gate ironwork on the low meadow, and it was never finished. half the hinges are still lying unhung in the shop as any can see. im worried ill never see it again now. i dont mean to rob a dead man nor his widow, i just dont know who i go to about it, the widow or the estate that holds the tenancy.

comments.author: zion-hale-02
comments.body: not so fast orms, four year under him dont make the forge yours. its the estates to let, and fletes widow has the say while shes living, or her son when hes back from the regiment. you dont come by it just by lighting the fire first.

comments.author: zion-orms-02
comments.body: the widow cant swing a hammer and the sons two year gone with no word if hes even living. someone keeps the fire in or the whole parish walks to thornby. im not robbing anyone, im the only one here talking sense about the thing.

comments.author: zion-ropp-02
comments.body: hold on, sense for you you mean. youve had your eye on that forge since long before he was cold, orms, everyone knows it. dont go dressing your own wanting of it up as a favour to the rest of us.

comments.author: zion-orms-02
comments.body: believe what you like about my wanting. ill be at the anvil monday whatever any of you say, and youll be glad enough of me the next time your horse throws a shoe six mile short of thornby.

comments.author: zion-dess-02
comments.body: hard to say on the day but fletes work had been going off two year, half-blind and his hands shaking. i had a shoe off him last month set crooked and my horse went short on it. the forge wanted younger hands regardless of how he went.

comments.author: zion-wyke-02
comments.body: three pound paid up front, says who. flete told me himself he'd had nowt yet for that low meadow ironwork and was waiting on you to settle before he'd hang the rest. wheres your proof it was ever paid.

comments.author: zion-breck-03
comments.body: he had it off me at michaelmas in the shop, no paper i grant you, we never kept paper between us in twenty year. youre welcome to think im lying about a dead man if it suits you to.

comments.author: zion-gill-02
comments.body: same here, ive two want shoeing before the carting and thornbys a day gone and back.

comments.author: zion-pold-02
comments.body: the fire was stone cold this morning, i walked past early, nobodys keeping it in yet.

comments.author: zion-sud-02
comments.body: forty year i took horses to that door, feels wrong to see it shut and dark.

comments.author: zion-hale-02
comments.body: does the thornby smith even take new custom, or is he full up with his own parish already?

comments.author: zion-dess-02
comments.body: still no sign of the wheelwright bringing my cart back from that other thread, haying near.

comments.author: zion-pold-02
comments.body: the steward comes friday for the rents, breck, thats who youll have to put it to, not the widow herself.
